mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate signals monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.68% as of Jan 2008 reflects Fed easing cycle
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4 percentage points in 2 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.5% as of Jan 2008, near cycle lows
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with emerging labor market stress
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts signal weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand contraction, which reduces construction employment and consumer wealth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1559k units as of Dec 2007, declining from prior peaks
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite housing starts decline, or correlation between HOUST changes and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth reflects real economic capacity utilization and forward earnings potential. Declining industrial production signals supply-side weakness and reduced profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day returns following negative INDPRO growth are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales and housing starts jointly capture consumer and investment demand. When both decline simultaneously, it signals broad-based demand destruction that compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth < 0 AND HOUST month-over-month growth < 0
evidence_refs: fact:Both RSAFS and HOUST showing weakness in late 2007
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction effect (joint signal) does not outperform individual series signals (Rank IC difference < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment and unemployment rate jointly capture labor market tightness. When payroll growth decelerates while unemployment rises, it signals accelerating labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth < 0.1% AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 1 month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 152.5M as of Jan 2008, UNRATE at 3.5%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of co-triggered instances, or the joint signal does not improve predictive power over individual components (Rank IC difference < 0.015).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI and monetary policy stance measured by federal funds rate jointly determine real interest rates. When CPI inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains low, it signals negative real rates that compress equity risk premiums, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors rotate into equities as inflation hedge.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 295.27 as of Jan 2008, FEDFUNDS at 1.68%
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 50% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction effect does not outperform individual series (Rank IC difference < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth captures aggregate demand strength. In late-cycle expansions, GDP growth deceleration below 2% annualized signals demand exhaustion and forward earnings contraction, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. This mechanism is regime-conditional: it activates only when the economy is already in late expansion (UNRATE < 4.5%).
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth < 2.0% AND UNRATE < 4.5%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19681.68B as of Q3 2007, UNRATE at 3.5%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window in late-cycle regimes (UNRATE < 4.5%).
